44.19: When they go out into the outer court where the people are, they shall take off their serving garments and leave them in the holy chambers, then put on other clothes, so that their sacred garments do not transfer holiness to the people.
44.20: They shall not shave their heads or let their hair grow long; they shall keep the hair of their heads trimmed.
44.21: No priest shall drink wine when entering the inner court.
44.22: They shall not marry a widow or a divorced woman, but only a virgin from the offspring of the house of Israel, or the widow of a former priest.
44.23: They shall teach my people the difference between the holy and the common, and show them how to distinguish between the unclean and the clean.
44.24: In a dispute they shall act as judges and decide according to my judgments. They shall keep my laws and my statutes in all my appointed feasts, and they shall keep my Sabbaths holy.
44.25: They shall not defile themselves by going near a dead person; however, for father or mother, son or daughter, brother or unmarried sister, they may defile themselves.
44.26: After he has been cleansed, they shall count seven days for him.
44.27: On the day that he enters the holy place — the inner court — to minister in the holy place, he shall offer his sin offering, declares the Lord GOD.
44.28: This shall be their inheritance: I am their inheritance. And you shall give them no possession in Israel; I am their possession.
44.29: They shall eat the grain offering, the sin offering, and the guilt offering, and every devoted thing in Israel shall be theirs.
44.30: And the first of all the firstfruits of every kind, and every offering of every kind from all your offerings, shall belong to the priests. You shall also give the priest the first portion of your dough, so that a blessing may rest on your house.
44.31: The priests shall not eat any bird or animal that has died of itself or been torn by wild animals.

45.1: When you divide the land as an inheritance, you shall set apart for the LORD a holy district twenty-five thousand cubits long and twenty thousand cubits broad; it shall be holy throughout its entire area.
45.2: Of this, a square of five hundred by five hundred cubits shall be for the sanctuary, with fifty cubits of open space all around it.
45.3: And from this district you shall measure off a section twenty-five thousand cubits long and ten thousand cubits broad, in which the sanctuary — the most holy place — shall be.
45.4: This shall be the holy portion of the land, belonging to the priests who minister in the sanctuary, who draw near to the LORD to minister to him. It shall be the place for their houses and a holy place for the sanctuary.
45.5: A portion twenty-five thousand cubits long and ten thousand cubits broad shall belong to the Levites, the ministers of the temple, as their possession for cities to live in.
45.6: Alongside the holy district you shall assign for the city a possession five thousand cubits broad and twenty-five thousand cubits long, corresponding to the full length of the holy district. It shall belong to the whole house of Israel.
45.7: To the prince shall belong land on both sides of the holy district and the city's holding — westward on the west and eastward on the east. In length it shall correspond to one of the tribal portions, from the western to the eastern boundary of the land.
45.8: This shall be his possession in Israel. And my princes shall no more oppress my people, but they shall give the house of Israel the land according to their tribes.
45.9: Thus says the Lord GOD: Enough, O princes of Israel! Put away violence and oppression, and execute justice and righteousness. Stop dispossessing my people, declares the Lord GOD.
45.10: You shall have honest scales, an honest ephah, and an honest bath.
45.11: The ephah and the bath shall be the same measure: the bath containing one-tenth of a homer and the ephah one-tenth of a homer; the homer shall be the standard measure.
45.12: The shekel shall be twenty gerahs; twenty shekels plus twenty-five shekels plus fifteen shekels shall be your mina.
45.13: This is the offering you shall make: one sixth of an ephah from each homer of wheat, and one sixth of an ephah from each homer of barley,
45.14: and as the fixed portion of oil, one tenth of a bath from each cor of oil (the cor, which is ten baths and equals one homer, for ten baths make a homer);
45.15: and one sheep from every flock of two hundred, from the well-watered pastures of Israel. These are for the grain offerings, burnt offerings, and peace offerings, to make atonement for the people, declares the Lord GOD.
45.16: All the people of the land shall be obligated to make this offering to the prince in Israel.
45.17: It shall be the prince's duty to provide the burnt offerings, grain offerings, and drink offerings at the feasts, the new moons, and the Sabbaths — all the appointed feasts of the house of Israel. He shall provide the sin offerings, grain offerings, burnt offerings, and peace offerings, to make atonement for the house of Israel.
45.18: Thus says the Lord GOD: In the first month, on the first day of the month, take a bull from the herd without blemish and cleanse the sanctuary.
45.19: The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering and put it on the doorpost of the temple, on the four corners of the altar's ledge, and on the posts of the inner court's gate.
45.20: You shall do the same on the seventh day of the month for anyone who has sinned unintentionally or through ignorance. So you shall make atonement for the temple.
45.21: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, you shall keep the Passover, a feast of seven days; unleavened bread shall be eaten.
45.22: On that day the prince shall provide for himself and for all the people of the land a bull as a sin offering.
45.23: On each of the seven days of the feast he shall provide seven bulls and seven rams without blemish as a burnt offering to the LORD each day, and a male goat daily for a sin offering.
45.24: He shall provide as a grain offering one ephah for each bull and one ephah for each ram, with a hin of oil for each ephah.
45.25: In the seventh month, on the fifteenth day of the month, at the feast, he shall provide the same for seven days — for the sin offering, the burnt offering, the grain offering, and the oil.

40.1: In the twenty-fifth year of our exile, at the start of the year, on the tenth of the month, fourteen years after the city had fallen — on that very day the hand of the LORD came upon me and brought me there.
40.2: In divine visions he brought me to the land of Israel and set me on a very high mountain, on whose southern slope was something resembling a city.
40.3: He brought me there, and I saw a man whose appearance was like gleaming bronze, holding a linen measuring cord and a measuring reed, standing in the gateway.
40.4: The man said to me, 'Son of man, look with your eyes, hear with your ears, and fix your attention on everything I am showing you — for you were brought here precisely so that I might show it to you. Report everything you see to the house of Israel.'
40.5: There was a wall surrounding the outside of the temple complex. The measuring reed in the man's hand was six long cubits in length, each cubit being a standard cubit plus a handbreadth. He measured the thickness of the wall: one reed; and its height: one reed.
40.6: He came to the east-facing gate, climbed its steps, and measured the threshold of the gate: one reed deep.
40.7: The guardrooms were each one reed long and one reed wide; the spaces between the guardrooms were five cubits; and the threshold by the gate's inner vestibule was one reed.
40.8: He measured the inner porch of the gate: one reed.
40.9: He measured the gate's entry porch: eight cubits; its doorposts: two cubits. The entry porch faced inward.
40.10: The guardrooms of the east gate — three on one side, three on the other — were all the same size; the doorposts on either side matched as well.
40.11: He measured the width of the gate opening: ten cubits, and the gate passage's total length: thirteen cubits.
40.12: A low barrier of one cubit stood in front of each guardroom on either side, and each guardroom measured six cubits square.
40.13: He measured the full width of the gate, ceiling to ceiling across the opposite guardrooms: twenty-five cubits, door facing door.
40.14: He made the doorposts sixty cubits high, and the court ran around the gate on every side.
40.15: From the outer face of the gate entrance to the inner face of the gate's vestibule: fifty cubits.
40.16: There were recessed windows facing inward in the guardrooms and their doorposts, and in the vestibules as well — windows all around the interior. Palm trees adorned each doorpost.
40.17: He brought me into the outer court; I saw chambers arranged all around and a pavement running the circuit, with thirty chambers fronting the pavement.
40.18: The pavement ran alongside the gates, matching their length; this was called the lower pavement.
40.19: He measured the distance from the front of the lower outer gate to the exterior of the inner court: a hundred cubits on the east and a hundred cubits on the north.
40.20: He then measured the length and width of the north-facing gate of the outer court.
40.21: The guardrooms — three on each side — and its doorposts and vestibule matched the first gate's dimensions: fifty cubits long, twenty-five cubits wide.
40.22: Its windows, vestibule, and palm trees matched the dimensions of the east gate; seven steps led up to it, with the vestibule in front.
40.23: The inner court had gates directly opposite the north and east outer gates; the measured distance from outer gate to inner gate was a hundred cubits.
40.24: He led me southward, where I saw a south gate; its doorposts and vestibule were measured according to the same standard.
40.25: It and its vestibule had windows all around, like the other gates, and measured fifty cubits long by twenty-five cubits wide.
40.26: Seven steps led up to it, the vestibule facing them; palm trees adorned its doorposts, one on each side.
40.27: There was a south-facing gate in the inner court; he measured a hundred cubits from outer gate to inner gate on the south side.
40.28: He brought me through the south gate into the inner court and measured the south gate; its measurements were the same as all the others.
40.29: Its guardrooms, doorposts, and vestibule matched the standard dimensions; it and its vestibule had windows all around — fifty cubits long, twenty-five cubits wide.
40.30: The vestibules around the gates were twenty-five cubits long and five cubits wide.
40.31: Its vestibule faced the outer court, with palm trees adorning its doorposts; its stairway had eight steps.
40.32: He brought me to the east side of the inner court and measured the east inner gate; it matched the standard dimensions.
40.33: Its guardrooms, doorposts, and vestibule matched the same standard dimensions; it and its vestibule had windows all around — fifty cubits long, twenty-five cubits wide.
40.34: Its vestibule faced the outer court, with palm trees on its doorposts on each side; its stairway had eight steps.
40.35: He brought me to the north inner gate and measured it; it was the same as all the others.
40.36: Its guardrooms, doorposts, and vestibule all measured the same; windows ran around all sides — fifty cubits long and twenty-five cubits wide.
40.37: Its vestibule faced the outer court, with palm trees on its doorposts on each side; its stairway had eight steps.
40.38: A chamber with its doorway was built beside the inner gate's doorposts, where the burnt offering was washed before presentation.
40.39: In the gate's vestibule, two tables stood on each side — four in total — on which the burnt offering, sin offering, and guilt offering were slaughtered.
40.40: On the outside, beside the porch at the entrance of the north gate, two tables stood on each side of the gate porch.
40.41: Four tables on each side of the gate — eight tables in total — on which the sacrificial animals were slaughtered.
40.42: The four tables for the burnt offering were of cut stone, each one and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide, and one cubit high. On them were laid the implements used for slaughtering the burnt offering and the sacrifice.
40.43: Double hooks, a handbreadth long, were fastened all around the inside walls, and the flesh of the offerings rested on the tables.
40.44: Outside the inner gate in the inner court were chambers for the singers: one beside the north inner gate facing south, and one beside the south inner gate facing north.
40.45: He said to me, 'This south-facing chamber is for the priests who are responsible for the care of the temple.'
40.46: The north-facing chamber is for the priests who have charge of the altar. These are the sons of Zadok — those Levites who draw near to the LORD to serve him.
40.47: He measured the inner court: a hundred cubits long and a hundred cubits wide — a perfect square. The altar stood in front of the temple.
40.48: He brought me to the temple vestibule and measured its doorposts: five cubits on each side. The width of the gate passage was three cubits on each side.
40.49: The vestibule was twenty cubits long and eleven cubits wide; steps led up to it, and columns flanked the doorposts on either side.

41.1: He brought me to the nave of the temple and measured the doorposts: six cubits wide on each side — that was the width of the tabernacle entrance.
41.2: The entrance was ten cubits wide; the sides of the entrance measured five cubits on each side. He measured the length of the nave: forty cubits; and its breadth: twenty cubits.
41.3: He went inside and measured the doorpost of the inner entrance: two cubits; the entrance itself: six cubits; and the breadth of the entrance: seven cubits.
41.4: He measured the inner room's length: twenty cubits; and its breadth: twenty cubits — the room in front of the nave. He said to me, 'This is the Most Holy Place.'
41.5: He measured the wall of the temple: six cubits thick; and the side chambers surrounding the temple: four cubits wide, all around.
41.6: The side chambers were arranged in three tiers, thirty to each tier. They set into the offsets of the temple wall all around so that they could be supported — but they were not fastened into the main temple wall itself.
41.7: The side chambers grew wider as they rose, because the winding stairway around the temple went upward and the temple was broader above; so one ascended from the lowest story to the highest by way of the middle.
41.8: I also saw the height of the temple complex all around: the foundations of the side chambers rose to a full reed, six cubits high.
41.9: The outer wall of the side chambers was five cubits thick, and the open space between the temple's side chambers and the outer buildings was twenty cubits wide, circling the temple.
41.10: Between the chambers and the house was a twenty-cubit width all around on every side.
41.11: The doors of the side chambers opened into the free space: one door facing north, another facing south; and the free space was five cubits wide all around.
41.12: The building that faced the separate yard on the west side was seventy cubits wide; its walls were five cubits thick on every side; and it was ninety cubits long.
41.13: He measured the temple: a hundred cubits long. The separate yard and the western building with its walls also measured a hundred cubits.
41.14: The width of the temple's front face and the separate yard on the east side was also a hundred cubits.
41.15: He measured the length of the western building, facing the separate yard behind the temple, along with its galleries on each side: a hundred cubits. This included the inner nave and the vestibules of the court.
41.16: The thresholds, recessed windows, and galleries — all three stories facing the threshold — were paneled with wood throughout, from floor to windows, and the windows were covered.
41.17: Above the doorway, in the inner room, and on the outside, and on all the walls inside and out — all was measured and paneled.
41.18: The paneling was carved with cherubim and palm trees: a palm tree between each pair of cherubim, and each cherub had two faces.
41.19: Each cherub had a human face toward the palm tree on one side and the face of a young lion toward the palm tree on the other side — carved throughout the entire temple.
41.20: From floor to above the doors, the walls were carved with cherubim and palm trees.
41.21: The doorposts of the nave were squared, and the appearance of the sanctuary's face matched that of the nave.
41.22: There was a wooden altar, three cubits high and two cubits long, with its corners, base, and walls all of wood. He said to me, 'This is the table that stands before the LORD.'
41.23: The nave and the Holy Place each had double doors.
41.24: Each double door had two leaves — two swinging panels for each door.
41.25: The doors of the nave were carved with cherubim and palm trees, matching the carvings on the walls. A canopy of thick wooden planks covered the outside of the vestibule.
41.26: Recessed windows and palm trees adorned both sidewalls of the vestibule and the side chambers of the house and the canopy beams.

42.1: He led me out into the outer court toward the north and brought me to the chambers that faced the separate yard and the building on the north side.
42.2: The building's length facing north was a hundred cubits, and its breadth fifty cubits.
42.3: Opposite the twenty-cubit space of the inner court and opposite the pavement of the outer court, balconies ran in three stories facing each other.
42.4: In front of the chambers, a passageway ran along the inside, ten cubits wide and a hundred cubits long, with the chamber doors facing north.
42.5: The upper chambers were shorter than the lower and middle ones, because the balconies ate into more of their space.
42.6: Because they were in three stories without pillars like those of the courts, the upper chambers were set back further from the ground than the lower and middle ones.
42.7: The outer wall running parallel to the chambers, facing the outer court on the front side, was fifty cubits long.
42.8: The chambers facing the outer court were fifty cubits long, while those facing the nave extended to a hundred cubits.
42.9: Below these chambers, on the east side, was an entrance for going into them from the outer court.
42.10: The chambers on the south side were set into the thickness of the court wall facing east, across from the separate yard and the western building.
42.11: The passageway before them was like that of the north chambers — the same length, the same width, the same exits, the same arrangements, and the same doors as those to the north.
42.12: Corresponding to the south chamber doors was a door at the top of the passageway — the pathway that ran directly beside the wall on the east — by which one entered.
42.13: He said to me, 'The north and south chambers facing the separate yard are holy chambers where the priests who approach the LORD shall eat the most holy offerings. There they shall deposit the most holy things — the grain offering, the sin offering, and the guilt offering — for the place is holy.'
42.14: 'When the priests enter the holy area, they must not go directly out into the outer court; they must first lay down their ministry garments — for these are holy — and put on other clothes before drawing near to the area designated for the people.'
42.15: When he had finished measuring the interior of the temple area, he led me out through the east gate and measured the complex all around.
42.16: He measured the east side with the measuring reed: five hundred reeds, measured all around.
42.17: He measured the north side: five hundred reeds by the measuring reed, all around.
42.18: He measured the south side: five hundred reeds by the measuring reed.
42.19: He turned to the west side and measured: five hundred reeds by the measuring reed.
42.20: He measured it on all four sides. A wall surrounded the entire complex — five hundred reeds on each side — to make a separation between the holy and the common.

46.1: Thus says the Lord GOD: The gate of the inner court that faces east shall remain shut on the six working days, but shall be opened on the Sabbath day and on the day of the new moon.
46.2: The prince shall enter through the vestibule of the gate from outside and stand by the gate post. The priests shall offer his burnt offering and his peace offerings, and he shall worship at the threshold of the gate. Then he shall go out, but the gate shall not be shut until evening.
46.3: The people of the land shall worship before the LORD at the entrance of that gate on the Sabbaths and new moons.
46.4: The burnt offering the prince shall offer to the LORD on the Sabbath shall be six lambs without blemish and one ram without blemish.
46.5: The grain offering with the ram shall be one ephah, and with the lambs as much as he is able to give, with a hin of oil to each ephah.
46.6: On the day of the new moon it shall be a young bull without blemish, six lambs and a ram, all without blemish.
46.7: He shall provide a grain offering of one ephah with the bull and one ephah with the ram, and with the lambs as much as he is able, with a hin of oil to each ephah.
46.8: When the prince enters, he shall go in through the vestibule of the gate and go out by the same way.
46.9: But when the people of the land come before the LORD at the appointed feasts, whoever enters by the north gate to worship shall go out by the south gate, and whoever enters by the south gate shall go out by the north gate. No one shall return through the gate by which he entered; each shall go out through the opposite gate.
46.10: The prince shall be among them; when they go in, he shall go in, and when they go out, he shall go out.
46.11: At the feasts and the appointed festivals, the grain offering shall be one ephah with a bull and one ephah with a ram, and with the lambs as much as one is able to give, with a hin of oil to each ephah.
46.12: When the prince provides a freewill offering — a burnt offering or peace offerings given freely to the LORD — the east gate shall be opened for him. He shall offer it as he does on the Sabbath day. Then he shall go out, and the gate shall be shut after he leaves.
46.13: You shall provide a yearling lamb without blemish as a daily burnt offering to the LORD; you shall provide it morning by morning.
46.14: You shall provide a grain offering with it every morning: one sixth of an ephah, with one third of a hin of oil to moisten the fine flour, as a grain offering to the LORD. This is a perpetual statute.
46.15: Thus the lamb, the grain offering, and the oil shall be provided morning by morning as a regular burnt offering.
46.16: Thus says the Lord GOD: If the prince makes a gift to any of his sons as an inheritance, it shall belong to his sons as their property by inheritance.
46.17: But if he makes a gift from his inheritance to one of his servants, it shall belong to him only until the year of liberty; after that it reverts to the prince. His inheritance may be given permanently only to his sons.
46.18: The prince shall not take any of the people's inheritance, dispossessing them of their property. He shall give his sons their inheritance out of his own property, so that none of my people is dispossessed of his property.
46.19: Then he brought me through the entrance beside the gate into the north row of the holy chambers for the priests; and I saw a place at the far western end.
46.20: And he said to me: This is where the priests shall boil the guilt offering and the sin offering and bake the grain offering, so that they need not carry them out into the outer court and thereby transmit holiness to the people.
46.21: Then he brought me out to the outer court and led me around to its four corners; and I saw that in each corner there was a small court.
46.22: In the four corners of the court were enclosed courts, forty cubits long and thirty cubits wide; all four were of the same dimensions.
46.23: There was a row of masonry around each of the four courts, with boiling places built at the base of the rows all around.
46.24: Then he said to me: These are the kitchens where those who serve at the temple shall boil the sacrifices of the people.

47.1: Then he brought me back to the entrance of the temple, and I saw water flowing out from below the threshold of the temple toward the east, for the temple faces east. The water flowed down from below the south end of the threshold, south of the altar.
47.2: He brought me out through the north gate and led me around on the outside to the outer gate that faces east; and I saw water trickling out from the south side.
47.3: As the man went eastward with his measuring line, he measured a thousand cubits and led me through the water; the water was ankle-deep.
47.4: Again he measured a thousand cubits and led me through the water; it was knee-deep. Again he measured a thousand cubits and led me through; it was waist-deep.
47.5: Again he measured a thousand cubits, and it was a river I could not cross, for the water had risen; it was deep enough to swim in, a river that could not be forded.
47.6: And he said to me: Son of man, have you seen this? Then he led me back to the bank of the river.
47.7: As I went back, I saw on the banks of the river a great many trees on both sides.
47.8: He said to me: These waters flow toward the eastern region and go down into the Arabah and enter the sea; when they flow into the sea, the water there will become fresh.
47.9: And wherever the river goes, every living creature that swarms will live; there will be very many fish. For these waters go there and make the sea fresh; everything will live where the river flows.
47.10: Fishermen will stand along it from En-gedi to En-eglaim; it will be a place for spreading nets. Its fish will be of very many kinds, like the fish of the Great Sea.
47.11: But its swamps and marshes will not become fresh; they will be left for salt.
47.12: On the banks of the river, on both sides, will grow all kinds of trees for food. Their leaves will not wither, nor their fruit fail; they will bear fresh fruit every month, because the water for them flows from the sanctuary. Their fruit will be for food, and their leaves for healing.
47.13: Thus says the Lord GOD: This is the boundary by which you shall divide the land as an inheritance among the twelve tribes of Israel. Joseph shall receive two portions.
47.14: You shall divide it equally, one share as well as another, for I raised my hand and swore to give it to your fathers; this land shall fall to you as your inheritance.
47.15: This shall be the northern boundary: from the Great Sea by way of Hethlon to Lebo-hamath, and on to Zedad,
47.16: Berothah, Sibraim (which lies on the border between Damascus and Hamath), as far as Hazer-hatticon, which is on the border of Hauran.
47.17: So the boundary shall run from the sea to Hazar-enan, at the northern border of Damascus, with the border of Hamath beyond. This shall be the north side.
47.18: On the east side: between Hauran and Damascus, and between Gilead and the land of Israel, the Jordan shall form the boundary down to the eastern sea. This shall be the east side.
47.19: The south side shall run from Tamar to the waters of Meribah-kadesh, and then along the Brook of Egypt to the Great Sea. This shall be the south side.
47.20: The west side: the Great Sea shall be the boundary, running to a point opposite the entrance of Hamath. This shall be the west side.
47.21: You shall divide this land among yourselves according to the tribes of Israel.
47.22: You shall allot it as an inheritance for yourselves and for the resident aliens who live among you and have had children among you. They shall be to you as native-born Israelites; they shall be allotted an inheritance among the tribes of Israel.
47.23: In whatever tribe the sojourner resides, there you shall assign his inheritance, declares the Lord GOD.

48.1: These are the names of the tribes. Starting at the northern extreme, from the road of Hethlon to Lebo-hamath, as far as Hazar-enan — on the northern border of Damascus, facing Hamath — from east to west: Dan, one portion.
48.2: Adjoining the territory of Dan, from the east side to the west side: Asher, one portion.
48.3: Adjoining the territory of Asher, from the east side to the west side: Naphtali, one portion.
48.4: Adjoining the territory of Naphtali, from the east side to the west side: Manasseh, one portion.
48.5: Adjoining the territory of Manasseh, from the east side to the west side: Ephraim, one portion.
48.6: Adjoining the territory of Ephraim, from the east side to the west side: Reuben, one portion.
48.7: Adjoining the territory of Reuben, from the east side to the west side: Judah, one portion.
48.8: Adjoining the territory of Judah, from the east side to the west side, shall be the sacred district you are to set apart: twenty-five thousand cubits in breadth, matching the tribal strips in length from east to west; the sanctuary shall be in the midst of it.
48.9: The portion you shall set apart for the LORD shall be twenty-five thousand cubits in length and twenty thousand cubits in breadth.
48.10: These shall be the allotments within the holy portion: the priests shall have a strip twenty-five thousand cubits long and ten thousand cubits wide, with the sanctuary of the LORD at the center.
48.11: This shall be for the consecrated priests, the sons of Zadok, who kept my charge and did not go astray when the people of Israel went astray, as the Levites did.
48.12: It shall be theirs as a special allotment from the holy district — a most holy section, adjoining the Levites' territory.
48.13: The Levites shall have an allotment alongside the priests: twenty-five thousand cubits in length and ten thousand in breadth. The total length shall be twenty-five thousand cubits and the breadth twenty thousand.
48.14: They shall not sell or exchange any of it; they shall not alienate this prime portion of the land, for it is holy to the LORD.
48.15: The remaining strip of five thousand cubits in breadth and twenty-five thousand in length shall be for common use: for the city, for housing and for open land. The city shall be in the midst of it.
48.16: These shall be the city's dimensions: on the north four thousand five hundred cubits, on the south four thousand five hundred, on the east four thousand five hundred, on the west four thousand five hundred.
48.17: The city shall have open land around it: two hundred and fifty cubits on the north, two hundred and fifty on the south, two hundred and fifty on the east, and two hundred and fifty on the west.
48.18: What remains of the length alongside the holy portion — ten thousand cubits to the east and ten thousand to the west — shall lie alongside the holy portion. Its produce shall be food for the workers of the city.
48.19: The workers of the city, from all the tribes of Israel, shall cultivate it.
48.20: The entire portion you shall set apart shall be twenty-five thousand cubits by twenty-five thousand cubits; you shall set apart the holy portion together with the city's property.
48.21: What remains on both sides of the holy portion and the city's property — the twenty-five thousand cubits toward the eastern border and westward toward the western border, running parallel to the tribal strips — shall belong to the prince. The holy portion with the temple sanctuary shall be in the midst.
48.22: Moreover, from the Levites' possession and the city's property, which lie within the prince's territory, the area between the border of Judah and the border of Benjamin shall belong to the prince.
48.23: As for the remaining tribes, from the east side to the west side: Benjamin, one portion.
48.24: Adjoining the territory of Benjamin, from the east side to the west side: Simeon, one portion.
48.25: Adjoining the territory of Simeon, from the east side to the west side: Issachar, one portion.
48.26: Adjoining the territory of Issachar, from the east side to the west side: Zebulun, one portion.
48.27: Adjoining the territory of Zebulun, from the east side to the west side: Gad, one portion.
48.28: Adjoining the territory of Gad on the south, the boundary shall run from Tamar to the waters of Meribah-kadesh, then along the Brook of Egypt to the Great Sea.
48.29: This is the land that you shall allot as an inheritance among the tribes of Israel, and these are their portions, declares the Lord GOD.
48.30: These shall be the exits of the city. On the north side, measuring four thousand five hundred cubits:
48.31: the gates of the city shall bear the names of the tribes of Israel — three gates on the north: the gate of Reuben, the gate of Judah, and the gate of Levi.
48.32: On the east side, measuring four thousand five hundred cubits: three gates — the gate of Joseph, the gate of Benjamin, and the gate of Dan.
48.33: On the south side, measuring four thousand five hundred cubits: three gates — the gate of Simeon, the gate of Issachar, and the gate of Zebulun.
48.34: On the west side, measuring four thousand five hundred cubits: three gates — the gate of Gad, the gate of Asher, and the gate of Naphtali.
48.35: The circumference of the city shall be eighteen thousand cubits. And the name of the city from that day on shall be: The LORD Is There.
